site.title: The Slow Table
site.description: Personal recipe book with an AI nutritionist

header.tagline: Your kitchen · your cookbook · your AI nutritionist

home.heroDescriptor: Build your own book of beautiful recipes — the comfort of home, daily ideas, and exact nutrition from AI.
home.heroCaptionTitle: Croissant with brie, plum and pistachio cream
home.editorP1: This is my thirteenth year in Prague. I left with a single suitcase and the fear that I’d never recreate the smell of home. So I began collecting recipes — typing them into the notes on my phone, photographing the pages of my mother’s notebook, coaxing my grandmother into explaining how she bakes that sorrel pie, saving finds from social media and rewriting them to fit my family. In time, people started asking me to “send the recipe.” This book is what it all became: a collection of family recipes, and my own, gathered along the way.
home.editorP2: No “healthy weight loss” here, no “boosted metabolism,” none of the advice you’ve heard a million times. Here is what I cook on weekdays and weekends: beautiful, delicious dishes for myself, the people I love, and guests — for when you want comfort, flavour, and the smell of something good. With stories. With an AI nutritionist that counts calories precisely, through USDA. And with a place where you can collect your own recipes.
home.editorSign: — Daria Bobina, editor. Prague, May MMXXVI.
home.pullQuote: Food is not fuel. It’s memories, love and care.
home.emptySubtitle: The most loved dishes will appear here soon — the ones made with soul and warmth.
home.statsTitle: Why this isn’t just another recipe collection.

stats.t: Nutrition accuracy. Through USDA mapping, not ChatGPT — which is off by 30%.
Welcome AI credits to start. Try all the magic without reaching for your card.
A month of Premium — lifts the limits, opens import, export, dark mode.
Lifetime seats for the first readers. One payment — forever.

home.subLede: Free — your book and ten AI credits to try. Premium lifts the ceilings: unlimited AI nutritionist, import a recipe from any site, export to PDF, dark mode and a week’s menu.
home.subLifetimeLede: One payment — Premium forever + 50 AI images. Your name in every issue’s colophon. Closes once all 50 seats are filled.

pricing.metaDescription: Free, Premium and Lifetime — choose how you read The Slow Table. A precise AI nutritionist, import, export and a week’s menu.
pricing.headerLede: Free — your book and ten AI credits to try. Premium lifts the ceilings and adds the nutrition magic. Lifetime — for the first fifty; one payment, forever. AI images are separate, by credits.

tiers.lede: Your book, to see whether you need more. No card, no trial, no reminders.
Unlimited AI, import, export, week’s menu. That “Notion from heaven”, just for food.
One payment, Premium forever. Your name in every issue’s colophon. Closes once it fills up.

pricing.aiLede: Your own style — top-down, warm light, a wooden board. One preset, the book’s recognizable visual DNA. Premium only, by credits only.
pricing.testimonialQuote: I paid for Notion templates and dropped them within a week. This is the first cooking subscription I’m not embarrassed about.
faq: Any EU/UK/US cards via Paddle (which also issues the invoice and pays VAT for us). Visa/Mastercard from Kazakhstan, Armenia, Georgia, Israel and the UAE work. Cards issued in Russia don’t yet — we’re looking at crypto, will let you know.
What if I subscribe and it’s not for me?
Cancel in one click — the unused part of the month isn’t refunded (that’s a standard subscription). For Lifetime — a refund within the first 30 days, no questions.
Does the AI really count accurately?

Accurate means ±5%. We map ingredients into USDA FoodData Central (a government database of ~400k products) and compute deterministically. This isn’t ChatGPT, which is off by 25-40%. If an ingredient isn’t found — we ask you, we don’t make it up.
Can I use the book with clients (I’m a coach)?
For now — personal use. A B2B plan is planned for months 10-12: PDF export with your brand, white-label client folders. If you need it urgently — write, we’ll talk.
What if my child has allergies?
AI recipe generation accounts for allergies and disliked foods (set it up once). Notes and filters — on all plans. We don’t give medical advice — that’s a legal boundary.
pricing.finalLede: Free gives you the book. Premium lifts the ceilings. Lifetime — for the first fifty. Choose.

recipe.servingsHeading: for {count, plural, one {# serving} other {# servings}}

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.method: Calculated via USDA FoodData Central — a government database of ~400,000 products. Accuracy ±5%, not “roughly what ChatGPT said”.
